consensus_trades.catalyst: Base effects from March oil spike and seasonal moderation; expected cooling of inflation due to Fed policy tightening
consensus_trades.risk_notes: Continued energy price volatility from Middle East situation could drive inflation higher. Current price 0.85 significantly exceeds max_acceptable_price 0.34—entry NOT recommended at current levels.

single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini analyzed 'Fed cuts before 2027' (opposite framing) as NO direction with different ticker and probability structure. Claude's zero-cuts contract is more granular and exploits tail hawkishness; Gemini's 'any cuts' formulation is less precise for this regime.

Gemini explicitly avoided weather microstates, citing insufficient grounding for precise prediction. Claude took calculated bet with 18% edge and medium conviction. Weather trades below macro/macro-adjacent focus but valid as uncorrelated diversifier.

contradictions.explanation: Both agree GDP will miss 2.0% threshold, but Claude targets 2.0% barrier (expecting ~1.3%) with prob_range [0.3, 0.45], while Gemini targets 1.5% barrier (different contract) with prob_range [0.55, 0.65]. These ranges do not overlap and represent different strike levels. Claude's 2.0% contract is more hawkish on growth slowdown. This is a range_mismatch on the same directional thesis but different granularity—skip as not true consensus.

portfolio_concentration_warning: CRITICAL: Consensus CPI trade (KXCPI-26APR-T0.4) assigned weight 6, causing inflation cluster to exceed cap of 5. Current market price 0.85 also SIGNIFICANTLY exceeds max_acceptable_price 0.34 (32.5% unfavorable to limit). RECOMMENDATION: Do NOT enter this trade at current price. If market reprices below 0.35, reduce weight to 4 and rebalance fed_policy down to 2 to stay within cluster limits. Alternatively, scale entire portfolio by 10% to reduce total inflation exposure relative to bankroll. Single-source fed_policy trade (weight 3) is well-grounded but in same cluster; combined fed_policy + potential GDP trades could approach cluster cap if GDP consensus forms.
reeval_triggers: CPI data release 2026-05-12: If April CPI >0.4% MoM, immediate reversal of consensus trade thesis
Fed FOMC Minutes or dot plot update: Any shift toward 1+ cuts in 2026 contradicts Claude single-source rate-cut-0 thesis
Q1 GDP advance estimate 2026-04-30: Resolution of GDP threshold contradiction; if Q1 prints >2.0%, both models wrong
Energy price spike >$90/bbl WTI: Could invalidate April CPI base-effects narrative
Labor market deterioration (NFP miss >100k two consecutive months): Could force Fed pivot and trigger rate cuts despite inflation
Weather forecast revisions for NYC: Resolution of April 13 high temp by 2026-04-14 EOD
